title: Flaky Italian Ricotta Tart
description: A buttery, flaky crust cradles a creamy ricotta filling spiced with vanilla, sundried tomato, spinach, and a splash of white wine for a subtle umami kick-perfect for a nutfree brunch.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk flour, salt, and ice water until a shaggy dough forms.
Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead just until it comes together, about 1 minute.
Roll the dough into a disc, wrap in plastic, and refrigerate for 20 min.

While the dough chills, heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 30 s. Add chopped spinach and sauté until wilted, about 2 min. Deglaze the pan with white wine, scraping up any browned bits. Let the mixture cool.

On a lightly floured surface, roll the chilled dough into an 8‑inch circle. Place the dough in a 9‑inch tart pan, pressing into the bottom and up the sides. Trim any excess dough. Prick the bottom with a fork to prevent bubbling.
In a medium bowl, combine ricotta, grated Parmesan, vanilla extract, eggs, salt, pepper, and the cooled spinach‑tomato mixture. Stir until smooth.
Pour the filling into the prepared crust, smoothing the top with a spatula.
Bake for 18–20 min, or until the crust is golden and the filling is set and lightly golden on top.
Remove from the oven and let the tart cool in the pan for 5 min, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely before slicing.
